raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Opioid-related overdoses claimed 3,244 lives in California in 2019.",
    "Opioid overdoses are a top five cause of death in the United States.",
    "The opioid and substance abuse epidemic has worsened during the COVID-19 pandemic.",
    "There has been a tragic rise in substance abuse and addiction since the start of the COVID-19 pandemic.",
    "Assembly Bill 77 aims to raise standards and protect patients in substance abuse treatment facilities.",
    "AB 1158 will require licensed drug abuse recovery and treatment facilities to maintain minimum insurance coverage levels.",
    "Senate Bill 434 prohibits false advertising or marketing services in licensed treatment facilities.",
    "SB 541 requires licensed treatment facilities to disclose their license number and expiration date in marketing materials.",
    "SB 349 creates a 'Bill of Rights' for clients of treatment programs to ensure they receive evidence-based treatment from qualified caregivers.",
    "SB 106 incentivizes the use of Mental Health Services funds for full-service partnerships in mental health care.",
    "AB 381 requires that licensed treatment facilities maintain at least two unexpired doses of naloxone on the premises.",
    "Assembly Bill 666 aims to address the shortage and lack of diversity within California's behavioral health workforce.",
    "AB 1011 prevents health plans from imposing prior authorization or step therapy requirements for FDA-approved substance use disorder treatments.",
    "AB 653 aims to create a Medication-Assisted Treatment Grant Program to award grants for substance abuse treatment."
  ]
}
```
